primary_segment: Rare earth mining, separation and NdFeB magnets
notes: Only vertically integrated US mine-to-magnet chain (Mountain Pass -> Fort Worth); DoD equity stake and price floor deal.; FY2025 10-K; net income attrib. to MP; mkt cap=shares x $45.70 (2026-07-20 close, Yahoo); R&D not separately reported
Largest rare earth producer outside China; Mt Weld mine, Malaysia cracking/separation plant, new Texas heavy rare earth facility underway.

primary_segment: Rare earth mining, processing and trading
notes: China's dominant rare earth ore aggregator/trader; holds an equity stake in MP Materials, linking Chinese and US supply.

primary_segment: Light rare earth mining and separation
notes: World's largest light rare earth producer, centered on Baotou; its quarterly transfer price sets China's domestic rare earth benchmark.
China's largest sintered NdFeB magnet maker, feeding EV traction motors, wind turbines and robotics; also dual-listed HKEX 06680; runs magnet recycling lines.
Holds foundational sintered NdFeB magnet patents; renamed from Hitachi Metals after a 2022 Bain Capital-led consortium buyout, now private.

primary_segment: Rare earth permanent magnets and soft magnetic alloys
notes: Century-old Western magnet maker building US capacity (South Carolina); Energy Fuels agreed June 2026 to acquire it for ~$1.9B, deal not yet closed as of foundation date.
Runs Silmet in Estonia, one of the very few commercial rare earth separation plants outside China; also makes magnetic powders/magnets in China.

primary_segment: Uranium mining and rare earth separation
notes: Operates White Mesa Mill (Utah), currently the only licensed rare earth separation facility on US soil; also dual-listed TSX: EFR; agreed to acquire VAC.; FY2025 10-K; mkt cap=shares x $11.74 (2026-07-20); R&D not separately reported

primary_segment: Rare earth mining and NdFeB magnets
notes: Developing the Round Top (Texas) heavy rare earth deposit and a Stillwater, Oklahoma magnet plant; early-stage, not yet a chokepoint at scale.

primary_segment: Mineral sands and rare earth refining
notes: Building the Eneabba rare earth refinery with an Australian government loan; not yet operating at commercial scale.

World's largest lithium producer by revenue; brine (Chile, Nevada) and hard-rock (Australia) feedstock plus global conversion capacity.; FY2025 10-K; net income attrib. to Albemarle; mkt cap=shares x $118.20 (2026-07-20)

company: SQM (Sociedad Quimica y Minera de Chile)
notes: One of the world's lowest-cost, largest lithium producers, drawing brine from the Atacama salt flat; also lists SQM-B on the Santiago exchange.; FY2024 20-F (FY2025 not yet filed); USD reporter; net income attrib. to owners; mkt cap & R&D not sourced (shares absent from XBRL facts; R&D not separately reported)

primary_segment: Lithium mining, conversion and battery materials
notes: Largest lithium compound producer, vertically integrated from mines/brines through lithium salts into cathodes and batteries; dual-listed HKEX 1772.
Major owner (~26%) of Greenbushes in Australia, the world's largest hard-rock lithium mine, plus China conversion capacity; dual-listed HKEX 9696.
Operates Pilgangoora, the largest independent hard-rock lithium mine; renamed from Pilbara Minerals to PLS Group in November 2025.
Developing Thacker Pass, Nevada, the largest known US lithium deposit, backed by a US Department of Energy loan; also dual-listed TSX: LAC.

primary_segment: Cobalt and copper mining and commodity trading
notes: Largest cobalt miner/trader globally via DRC copper-cobalt operations (Katanga, Mutanda); also the biggest independent commodity trading house.
World's largest cobalt producer by volume via DRC's Tenke Fungurume and Kisanfu mines; also dual-listed HKEX 03993.

primary_segment: Cobalt/nickel refining and battery precursor materials
notes: Dominant refiner turning DRC cobalt ore into battery-grade sulfates and precursor cathode active material (pCAM); controls a key node most DRC cobalt must pass through.
Largest refined nickel and palladium producer with meaningful cobalt byproduct; sanctions exposure, but Indonesian nickel supply offers a substitute path.

primary_segment: Iron ore, nickel and cobalt mining
notes: Major diversified nickel/cobalt producer across Brazil, Canada and Indonesia; home listing is B3 (VALE3), ADR trades as NYSE: VALE.; FY2025 20-F; USD reporter; net income attrib. to Vale stockholders; capex=IFRS PP&E additions (cash-flow capex not separately tagged); mkt cap=shares outstanding x $14.10 (2026-07-20)

primary_segment: Battery materials recycling and precursor/cathode manufacturing
notes: Leading European battery metals recycler and cathode precursor maker; closes the loop by recovering cobalt, nickel and lithium from end-of-life batteries.

primary_segment: Diversified mining incl. nickel and potash
notes: Diversified miner with Western Australia Nickel operations (currently curtailed on low prices) and a growing potash business; dual-listed LSE/NYSE.

primary_segment: Diversified mining incl. lithium and borates
notes: Dual-listed company (plc London / Limited Melbourne) developing lithium (Rincon, Argentina; Jadar, Serbia) and producing scandium as a byproduct; also ASX: RIO and NYSE ADR.

primary_segment: Natural graphite mining and anode material
notes: Runs Balama, Mozambique, one of the world's largest natural graphite mines, plus the Vidalia, Louisiana anode material plant, a rare non-China graphite-to-anode chain.

primary_segment: Zinc mining and germanium/gallium byproduct refining
notes: Major Chinese germanium producer (byproduct of zinc smelting); germanium and gallium have been under Chinese export licensing controls since August 2023.
